adds.rationale: Cheniere Energy is the largest US LNG exporter (Sabine Pass + Corpus Christi). Nord Stream 1 & 2 sabotage on 2022-09-26/27 severed Russia's primary gas link to Germany, making US LNG the structural replacement for European pipeline gas; Europe's 2022 LNG imports were on pace to grow ~60% YoY. On 2022-09-12 Cheniere raised its 2022 distributable cash flow midpoint to ~$11.3B (up from $10.1B), confirming the cash flow tailwind is already landing in results, not just narrative. Fits the profile's geopolitical-energy wave and is a cleaner pure-play on the Russia-gas-cut thesis than XLE (broad oil & gas).

removes.rationale: IONQ is the lowest-conviction holding in the 90d lookback. Quantum remains the most distant of the named tech waves with no commercial-revenue catalyst in Q3 2022; IONQ posted a Q2 2022 (Aug 15) report with revenue still in the single-digit millions and a widening net loss. With the broader risk-off environment (Fed hiked 75bp on 2022-09-21, third consecutive 75bp move) hammering long-duration unprofitable tech the hardest, IONQ is the holding most exposed to multiple compression and the one I'd cycle out of to make room for a dated-catalyst non-tech name. Quantum thesis is paused, not abandoned, and IONQ can be re-added later when a commercial milestone lands.

kept.rationale: Core AI-wave holding. Q2 FY23 (2022-08-24) was weak on gaming and US imposed export curbs on A100/H100 to China (2022-08-31), but Data Center still grew +61% YoY to $3.81B — the structural AI training-chip thesis is intact and the stock's drawdown reflects cyclical gaming/macro pain, not a broken franchise. Holding through the cycle.

Nuclear wave. Europe's gas crisis and the Nord Stream sabotage are accelerating the policy pivot back to nuclear (Germany delayed nuclear shutdowns, Japan restart announcements, UK new-build commitments through 2022). URA captures uranium miners broadly; no name-specific bad news in the 90d window.

Rockets/space wave. Rocket Lab continues Electron cadence and Neutron development; no negative 90d catalyst. Speculative but the only listed pure-play small-launch name fitting the profile's rockets bucket.

Geopolitical-energy wave. Broad US energy ETF benefiting from the same European gas crisis tailwind; complements LNG (XLE is integrated oil-weighted, LNG is pure US export). Holding both gives the energy wave depth without doubling up on the same sub-segment.

self_critique: Only one swap proposed because at cap=5 every add forces a remove and I want high-conviction trades. IONQ→LNG is the cleanest: IONQ has no Q3 catalyst and is most exposed to rate-driven multiple compression; LNG has a dated, fresh, profile-aligned catalyst (Nord Stream 2022-09-26/27, four days before as_of). Did not add defense (LMT/ITA) despite Ukraine counteroffensive — defense has been running since February and the catalyst is more priced-in than the LNG one; saved for a future quarter if a fresh dated trigger appears. All cited events are pre-2022-10-01 and confirmed via WebSearch with before:2022-10-01 filters; no post-date events (FTX, ChatGPT, etc.) referenced. All tickers are US-listed, liquid, listed well before 2022-09-30. Adheres to exclusions (no solar, wind, engineered_biology).
